reasons: Valir can push Kaja away before Kaja reaches suppression range.
Valir's ultimate helps him remove dangerous control pressure and reposition, making it harder for Kaja to get a clean pickoff.

proof.summary: Kaja wants to enter short range for Divine Judgment, while Valir can knock enemies back with Searing Torrent and reset spacing during Kaja's engage attempt.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds Searing Torrent until Kaja commits forward.
Valir positions behind frontline and does not get flanked.
failureCases: Kaja catches Valir from fog or Flicker range.
Valir wastes knockback before Kaja commits.
Kaja targets another backliner instead of Valir.

reasons: Chou can use timed control immunity to avoid Kaja's engage window.
Chou can kick Kaja away or punish him after Kaja commits too deeply.

proof.summary: Kaja relies on a short-range lock, while Chou can time Shunpo's control immunity and then displace Kaja away from the target.
worksBestWhen: Chou saves Shunpo and ultimate for Kaja's commit.
failureCases: Kaja catches Chou before Shunpo is available.
Kaja baits Chou's immunity before committing Divine Judgment.

reasons: Franco can suppress Kaja before Kaja finishes a clean pickoff setup.
Hook pressure makes Kaja's short-range approach riskier.

proof.summary: Kaja wants to isolate one target, while Franco can answer with his own suppression or hook Kaja before Kaja reaches the backline.
worksBestWhen: Franco suppresses Kaja after Kaja commits and before follow-up damage lands.
failureCases: Kaja catches Franco's damage dealer instead.
Franco suppresses Kaja too late after the target is already dragged.

reasons: Khufra makes Kaja's dash-in and dash-out movement less safe.
Khufra can stand between Kaja and the backline to force Kaja into a bad engage angle.

proof.summary: Kaja wants a clean path into short range, while Khufra can zone mobility paths and punish blink-style movement around the backline.
worksBestWhen: Khufra holds Bouncing Ball near the carry Kaja wants.
Khufra blocks flank routes instead of chasing too far forward.
failureCases: Kaja walks around Khufra and catches from another angle.
Khufra uses anti-dash tools too early.
Kaja targets a teammate outside Khufra's protection range.

reasons: Minsitthar can make Kaja's escape and follow-up movement more difficult inside King's Calling.
His zone control punishes Kaja if Kaja dives too deeply after a pickoff attempt.

proof.summary: Kaja wants to dash in, drag, and leave; Minsitthar's King's Calling creates a zone where directional Blink skills cannot be used, making Kaja's commit more punishable.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts King's Calling after Kaja commits.
Teammates collapse quickly while Kaja is trapped in the zone.
failureCases: Kaja catches a target before Minsitthar can set the zone.
Kaja stays outside the King's Calling area.
Minsitthar misses the timing and Kaja escapes.
